shoulders: Tuck elbows (about 45°) and keep shoulders set back.
Ensure your feet are driven firmly into the ground.
Lower the bar to your lower chest/sternum, not your neck.
wrists: Stack wrists over elbows and keep knuckles up.
Squeeze the bar as hard as possible to stabilize the joint.
Check that your thumb is wrapped around the bar for safety.
none: Form looks solid. Focus on driving through your feet.
Keep your shoulder blades retracted throughout the set.
Maintain a slight arch in your lower back while keeping glutes on the bench.

lower_back: Brace your core harder and avoid 'butt wink' at the bottom.
Ensure your chest stays up and doesn't tip forward.
Check if your stance is too narrow, which can force back arching.
knees: Push your knees out and keep your weight on your mid-foot.
Avoid letting your knees cave inward during the ascent.
Try to sit back into the squat rather than just dropping down.
none: Great depth. Maintain a proud chest throughout the lift.
Continue to drive through the mid-foot.
Keep your neck neutral by looking at a spot on the floor 5 feet ahead.

lower_back: Hinge at the hips and keep the bar close to your shins.
Engage your lats by trying to 'protect your armpits' before lifting.
Ensure your hips don't rise too fast at the start of the pull.
grip: Try using chalk or a mixed grip for heavier sets.
Hold the bar in your fingers rather than deep in your palm to avoid tears.
Focus on 'crushing' the bar with your grip before the lift begins.
none: Solid lockout. Keep pulling through the heels.
Maintain a neutral spine from head to hips.
Take a big 'belly breath' and brace before each rep.

shoulders: Keep your core tight and avoid shrugging your shoulders at the start.
Keep your elbows slightly tucked in front of the bar.
Press the bar up and slightly back to finish over your mid-foot.
lower_back: Avoid excessive arching; squeeze your glutes to stabilize your spine.
Take a smaller step or keep feet shoulder-width for better balance.
Keep your ribs pulled down towards your pelvis.
none: Strong overhead position. Keep the bar path vertical.
Squeeze the bar hard to maintain tension.
Full lockout at the top with a 'shrug' to engage upper traps.

lower_back: Maintain a flat back and pull the bar towards your lower stomach.
Slightly bend your knees to take tension off the hamstrings.
Avoid using momentum or 'jerking' the weight up.
elbows: Lead the pull with your elbows and avoid 'flailing' them out.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Keep your wrists straight; avoid curling them at the end of the rep.
none: Good squeeze at the top. Keep your neck in a neutral position.
Focus on pulling with your back, not just your arms.
Control the weight on the way down for better muscle engagement.
